steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the function of the underlined sentence within the text as a whole. The first sentence establishes a general trend: in a boat, each crew member believes he alone is doing all the work. The underlined sentence then presents Harris's specific belief that he alone worked while George and the narrator imposed on him. The answer must capture how that sentence relates to what came before it.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'It establishes Harris's feelings about the narrator's idea of how boat trips usually proceed.': the underlined sentence is not Harris's reaction to the narrator's idea — it is an instance of the very pattern the narrator describes. ✗
  • Choice B — 'It offers Harris's belief as a specific example of a trend the narrator has observed while boating.': Harris's belief (he alone worked) is precisely one instance of the general trend (each crew member thinks he is doing everything). ✓
  • Choice C — 'It suggests that Harris is focused on helping the group navigate a challenge.': Harris believes he is the only one working — not that he is trying to help the group navigate anything. ✗
  • Choice D — 'It demonstrates that Harris finds his experiences on the boat to be confusing.': Harris is not confused — he holds a firm, confident belief. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
